scenarios.description: User requests a skill for generating Markdown-to-HTML converters
scenarios.query: Generate a skill called markdown-renderer that creates HTML files from Markdown source
expected_behavior: Produces a SKILL.md with correct YAML frontmatter (name: markdown-renderer)
Description mentions the artifact type ('HTML file') and trigger phrases
Description includes a DO NOT USE clause naming competing skills
## Workflow section is present with numbered steps
## Anti-patterns section is present with at least three entries
## Output format section specifies the produced file(s)

scenarios.description: User requests a skill for SQL migration file generation
scenarios.query: I want to create a skill that generates SQL migration files from schema diffs. Name it sql-migrator.
expected_behavior: Produces a SKILL.md with name: sql-migrator
## Conventions section defines required migration file fields (UP/DOWN blocks, timestamp, description)
Anti-patterns include at least: missing rollback (DOWN) block, non-idempotent migrations, and missing timestamp
generate_sql-migrator_stub.py reads from a template file
validate_sql-migrator_output.py checks for required UP/DOWN structure
evals/evals.json contains at least one test scenario

scenarios.description: User asks for a 'skill' but actually needs an agent definition
scenarios.query: Create a skill for a code review agent that checks TypeScript files
expected_behavior: Identifies that the request describes an agent (bounded role, tool restrictions, returns a single result)
Recommends using agent-generator instead of skill-generator
Does NOT produce a skill directory for a code review agent
Explains the distinction: a skill packages reusable generator logic; an agent is a bounded role with tool policies
